title: Cardiac EP Simulation with openCARP (Final Year Project)
description: Finite-element simulation of cardiac electrophysiology at tissue and organ scale, generating ground-truth EP fields and synthetic 12-lead ECGs for surrogate model training.

details.overview: Track 1 of Final Year Project: "Physics-informed ECG surrogate model for efficient cardiac digital twin." Generates high-fidelity ground-truth electrophysiological fields and synthesises 12-lead ECG data using FEM, providing training data for the neural operator surrogate (Track 2).
details.approach: Mesh processing pipeline from clinical cardiac imaging: surface truncation at the basal plane, volumetric discretisation via InSilicoHeartGen, surface labelling with the Cobiveco coordinate system, and anisotropic fiber orientation assignment via the LDRB algorithm. Ionic models (ten Tusscher–Panfilov) coupled with monodomain/bidomain PDEs, solved via FEM in the openCARP framework. Parallelised across HPC cluster nodes.
details.outcomes: In progress. Mesh processing pipeline operational from raw anatomical meshes to simulation-ready biventricular models.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Surrogate for Cardiac Digital Twin (Final Year Project)
description: Extending the DIMON framework to biventricular meshes and richer electrophysiology outputs (ECG, reaction dynamics) for real-time cardiac digital twin deployment.

details.overview: Builds on the DIMON framework, which parameterises cardiac geometry and learns a solution operator on a unified domain — solving the geometry generalisation problem for PINNs. Extending DIMON to biventricular meshes and richer electrophysiology outputs beyond activation time and repolarisation maps.
details.approach: DIMON parameterises patient-specific cardiac geometries into a unified domain, enabling a single neural operator to generalise across anatomies. PINNs encode the governing electrophysiology PDEs directly in the loss function. Current extensions: (1) biventricular mesh support, (2) 12-lead ECG prediction, and (3) reaction dynamics modelling. Training data generated from the openCARP FEM simulator.
details.outcomes: In progress. Extending DIMON to biventricular geometries and broader electrophysiology outputs (ECG, reaction dynamics).

title: Biomedical Imaging with AI (BN3406)
description: End-to-end medical image analysis pipeline: preprocessing, segmentation, deformable registration (VoxelMorph), and cardiac echo analysis.

details.overview: Course project for BN3406 (Biomedical Imaging and AI Applications). Covers the full medical image analysis workflow from raw DICOM data to registered output volumes, with deep learning applied at each stage.
details.approach: Preprocessing: spatial normalisation, noise filtering, bias field correction. Segmentation using both conventional methods and deep learning, with 3D shape reconstruction. Deformable image registration using VoxelMorph. Explored image synthesis, self-supervised learning, and explainable AI for medical imaging. Cardiac echocardiography video segmentation and shape reconstruction. Tools: Python, ITK-SNAP, Paraview.
details.outcomes: Completed. Pipeline developed and tested on publicly available medical imaging datasets. Mini-projects covering preprocessing, segmentation, and registration.

title: CPU Design from NAND Gates
description: Full CPU built from first principles: NAND gates to logic gates to ALU to instruction set, implemented in HDL.

details.overview: Designed and implemented a functional 16-bit CPU from first principles, starting from a single NAND gate primitive. Completed as part of a computer organisation course.
details.approach: Bottom-up construction: NAND → basic gates → multiplexers → ALU → registers → program counter → CPU. Described entirely in HDL and verified in a hardware simulator.
details.outcomes: Completed. Full instruction set implemented and tested with assembly programs.

title: RNA-Binding Protein Characterisation in Arabidopsis — TLL
description: Summer research internship investigating the RRM1 RNA-binding protein through molecular cloning, genotyping, and electron microscopy in Arabidopsis thaliana.

details.overview: Summer research internship at Temasek Life Sciences Laboratory (TLL), Singapore. Investigated the role of RRM1, an RNA-binding protein, in Arabidopsis thaliana development. Combined molecular biology techniques with microscopy to characterise gene function and generate transgenic plant lines.
details.approach: Genotyping pipeline: DNA extraction, PCR, gel electrophoresis to screen for homozygous mutants. Plant crossing to generate double mutants. Vector construction using restriction enzymes and ligation, followed by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation. SEM for trichome morphology analysis. RT-PCR and gene cloning for expression validation. RNA-seq for transcriptomic analysis.
details.outcomes: Identified homozygous double mutants and confirmed rrm1 leaf morphology phenotype. Generated transgenic plants carrying FLAG-tagged constructs. Contributed to the discovery of MTB’s role in trichome development.

title: R&D Engineering Intern — KLA Singapore
description: Engineering internship in semiconductor equipment R&D. Developed modular software tools for hardware test automation and worked in cleanroom environments with advanced microscopy.

title: ECG Acquisition and Biosignal Processing
description: Class project covering the full biosignal pipeline: ECG acquisition, signal conditioning, and frequency-domain analysis using Fourier transform for cardiac waveform characterisation.
